site.title: Moutia Benachour — Startup Systems Engineer
site.description: Full-stack engineer specializing in transactional correctness, financial infrastructure, and startup architecture.
site.author: Moutia Benachour

hero.name: Moutia Benachour
phrases: I build financial infrastructure for startups.
I architect systems that handle real money.
I help startups scale past their first architecture.
I turn ambiguous requirements into working systems.

descriptionParts.text: . I build the systems that move real money, survive scaling pressure, and stay maintainable under startup velocity.
. Available for full-time roles, consulting engagements, and technical advisory work through Toptal or directly.

availability.description: Full-time, consulting, and advisory positions. Especially in fintech, SaaS, and startups moving to their next stage. Remote worldwide, available from 6:30 PM UTC.

capabilitiesSection.label: What I bring
capabilitiesSection.subtitle: Not a list of technologies. Six capability areas that map directly to the problems startups face when they grow past their first system.

capabilities.title: Financial Infrastructure
capabilities.body: Double-entry ledgers, wallet systems, cent-accurate reconciliation, idempotent payment workflows. I build the financial layer that auditors and regulators can trust.

capabilities.title: Transactional Systems
capabilities.body: Idempotent workflows, retry pipelines, event-sourced state, queue-based processing. Systems that stay correct under failures, retries, and high concurrency.

capabilities.title: Startup Architecture
capabilities.body: Monorepo structure, API-first design, shared packages, deployment pipelines. Architecture that lets small teams move fast without accumulating dangerous debt.

capabilities.title: Platform Modernization
capabilities.body: Legacy system evolution without risky rewrites. I stabilize what exists, incrementally upgrade what matters, and replace what can't scale — pragmatically.

capabilities.title: Data & Automation
capabilities.body: Scraping pipelines, batch processing, ETL workflows, report generation. Reliable data infrastructure that doesn't collapse when the volume triples overnight.

capabilities.title: AI-Assisted Products
capabilities.body: Embeddings pipelines, semantic clustering, LLM-assisted features with deterministic fallbacks. AI that ships as product, not a demo — with validation and observability.

stagesSection.label: Career history
stagesSection.subtitle: Each role mapped to the startup lifecycle phase it represents — because the challenges of 0→1 are completely different from 10→100.

stages.label: Trading Platform
stages.outcomesTitle: What I built & why it matters
outcomes: Architected a Yarn 4 monorepo with shared packages for auth, UI, security, and observability — giving a small team the structure of a larger engineering org.
Built double-entry ledger and wallet systems with deposits, withdrawals, reserved balances, fees, and reconciliation-safe accounting — the financial backbone the platform runs on.
Implemented trading, IPO precommitment, NAV, and membership billing — complex financial workflows that require correctness, not just functionality.
Established AGENTS.md and repo-level AI documentation — making Claude Code a reliable team member with deterministic, auditable behavior.

stages.label: BNPL Platform
stages.outcomesTitle: What I built & why it matters
outcomes: Architected a complete BNPL platform — backend, cross-platform mobile app, admin CRM, and marketing site — from zero in a monorepo.

Designed the full lending lifecycle — investor funding, plan creation, virtual card issuance via Stripe Issuing, installment scheduling, delinquency, recovery, and expiry — all auditable and cent-accurate.
Built immutable investor and platform ledgers with wallet accounting, repayment distribution, profit-share calculations — financial infrastructure that scales to thousands of concurrent loans.
Implemented idempotent webhook ingestion and auditable workflow routing — making the system safe to retry under any failure condition.

stages.label: Dromigo
stages.outcomesTitle: What I built & why it matters
outcomes: Co-founded the company and shaped product vision and technical execution from ideation through exit.
Built a GIS-based freight matching engine — geospatial search connecting carriers and shippers across a two-sided logistics marketplace.
Shipped a Flutter mobile app with real-time driver tracking and Stripe payment integration.

stages.label: BenchCommerce
stages.outcomesTitle: What I built & why it matters
outcomes: Built an embeddings pipeline that vectorizes Shopify and Etsy product catalogs, stores vectors in PostgreSQL, and clusters products into semantic niches using cosine-similarity K-means.
Integrated an LLM naming layer with strict output validation and deterministic fallback — making AI-generated taxonomy stable and safe for business users to rely on.
Computed niche confidence scores using embedding cohesion, coverage, and representative-product agreement — turning black-box clustering into an observable, debuggable system.

stages.label: PowerMarket
stages.outcomesTitle: What I built & why it matters
outcomes: Upgraded deprecated Elastic Beanstalk infrastructure and modernized legacy packages — stabilizing a platform under scaling pressure without a risky full rewrite.
Optimized large-marker map performance using server-side and client-side caching with dynamic grouping by zoom level — solving a UX problem that had been blocking adoption.
Migrated mapping from Mapbox to Google Maps under deadline and improved scalability using queue-based workflows — two high-risk deliveries shipped on time.

stages.label: Laravel / Scraping
stages.outcomesTitle: What I built & why it matters
outcomes: Built resilient state-specific license scrapers with batching, retry/recovery, resume support, duplicate detection, and failure classification — turning fragile scripts into a reliable pipeline.
Modernized Laravel 8 toward a current stack — Jetstream, Livewire, Fortify auth, team management, admin/editor roles — incrementally, without breaking production.

stages.label: Toptal
outcomes: Vetted in the top 3% of engineers on Toptal — a rigorous screening process covering technical depth, communication, and delivery reliability.
Delivered complete products across fintech, energy, logistics, and e-commerce — working directly with founders, CTOs, and product owners across ambiguous, fast-moving environments.

projects.title: Dromigo
projects.description: Two-sided logistics marketplace with GIS freight matching, real-time driver tracking, and Stripe payments. Co-founded and shipped from zero.

projects.title: Rassidi
projects.description: Fintech PWA for virtual payment cards in local currency. Laravel API, real-time inventory, push notifications, and seamless mobile experience.

projects.title: Adgon Expert
projects.description: Full CRM for expert geometers with a public-facing professional search engine, subscription billing, and tender/news feeds per plan tier.

projects.title: Medic Dz
projects.description: Real-time medical appointment management. Patients and providers see live availability via WebSocket. Packaged as iOS and Android via Cordova.

aiSection.label: AI systems
aiSection.subtitle: AI features are useful only when they are reliable, observable, and designed with fallbacks. This is the operating style I use for embeddings, clustering, and LLM-assisted workflows.
strengths: Embeddings pipelines with batching, retry, and storage
Semantic clustering and vector similarity workflows
LLM-assisted product features with output validation
Deterministic fallback design for AI instability
AI-assisted engineering via Claude Code and AGENTS.md
Confidence scoring for observable AI systems
deepening: Agentic patterns and multi-step agent orchestration
RAG pipelines with retrieval quality evaluation
aiSection.quote: I treat AI features as product systems, not demos — with validation, fallbacks, and the same operational discipline I apply to financial workflows.

contact.copy: Open to full-time roles, consulting engagements, and technical advisory positions — especially in fintech, SaaS, and startups moving to their next stage. Remote worldwide.
